Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0x0400 "AlternativeFormats\PRGs\Uncrunched\Starace.prg,0x0400,0x00EC" -Di_irq_exit=0 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 lda #$34 sta $01 jsr $d800 lda #$35 sta $01 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Starace.prg"
Metadata.Notes:
As this game is actually frozen, the game starts at $359F (IRQ) / $0D2C (code). However the true start address is $0400.
Because of this, the JSR $04ED instruction at $0404 has been patched to be JSR $050F instead. This is done so that the first part of the init routine at $04ED is skipped, as otherwise data used for colour RAM gets wiped by the code being executed a second time (this code would have originally been called before the freeze was taken, meaning the data in RAM is setup as needed already).
Note: the loading screen by Stuart Fotheringham was obtained from the Games That Weren't website, as it was not included with the Zzap! covertape release. (https://www.gamesthatwerent.com/gtw64/starace).
